wards and provide for the represen-
tation thereof in the city council;
provided each ward shall have the
same number of councilmen as pro-
vided herein, and there shall be no
redistricting within three years
from the adoption of this charter.
and the term of Office as provided
in this charter shall not be
changed; provided further, there
shall not be more than 81 members
of the council until the population
of the city reaches 100000


Because OF partisan opposition
trated. The introduction of the
resolution to redistrict the city last
Tuesday night was taken with a
view of entirely eliminating politics
from the proposition. Everyone adj
mits the necessity OF greater
number of councilmen than were
required when the charter was
adopted, II years ago. At present
councilmen cannot take care of the
wishes or necessities of their con-
stituents with credit to themselves
and with justice to the latter. as
they could do If each ward had
from one fourth 10 one eighth of
the population that councilmen are
now required to represent.


There is another matter that has
some bearing in this connection,
that is, the much talked of council
man at large idea. | believe that
there should be such an Official,
and If there is no provision In the
city charter allowing the selection
of such an official by the council, I
think there should be passed at the
next city election, an amendment
10 the charter providing for the
election by the people of a council
man at large who would also act al
president of the city council. This
would give an odd number of menu
bers in that body and eliminate the
chance of tie vote, and further.
more, would preclude the pos
sibility of change In the wishes Of
the people as expressed in their
choice for mayor. in the succession
of the president of the city council
TO the mayorality II the mayor
should leave Office or die. When a
mayor was elected ON certain prin.
ciples and platforms, the people
would have chance to give an ex-
preston as to who his successor
should be and their wishes carried
out until the end of the term for
which the mayor was elected"
